cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-799 - Improper Control of Interaction Frequency

threat.title: Kubernetes Resource Quota Bypass via Unbounded Container Resources
threat.description: When Kubernetes namespaces lack ResourceQuota and LimitRange policies, attackers can deploy containers without resource limits, leading to cluster-wide resource exhaustion. This enables denial of service through CPU/memory bombs, fork bombs, PID exhaustion, ephemeral storage abuse, and /dev/shm attacks that can crash entire nodes or the control plane.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker gains access to Kubernetes cluster with namespace creation or pod deployment privileges
2. Identifies namespaces without ResourceQuota or LimitRange policies using kubectl get resourcequota --all-namespaces
3. Deploys malicious pod with no resource limits (unlimited CPU/memory requests)
4. Pod consumes all available node resources through fork bomb or memory allocation loop
5. Node becomes unresponsive, triggering kubelet eviction of other pods
6. Spreads attack across multiple namespaces or uses DaemonSets to impact all nodes
7. Cluster control plane becomes degraded or fails due to API server overload
8. Legitimate workloads crash, causing production outage
prerequisites: Kubernetes cluster without default ResourceQuota in namespaces
Missing LimitRange configuration for default resource limits
Attacker has pod creation permissions (via RBAC misconfiguration)
No PodSecurityPolicy or PodSecurityStandards enforcing resource limits
Insufficient node-level resource monitoring or alerting

cve_references.description: Kubernetes API server vulnerable to YAML bomb DoS attack via recursive parsing, allowing malicious YAML payloads to consume excessive CPU and memory
Container breakout allowing privilege escalation to host, enabling unlimited resource consumption outside container limits
cgroup kernel vulnerability allowing container escape to consume host resources and bypass cgroup limits

real_world_breaches.impact: Complete payment platform outage for 3+ hours affecting 2 million customers; card payments and ATM withdrawals failed
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Single container consumed excessive CPU resources due to missing resource limits, cascading failure across Kubernetes cluster

real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers deployed cryptomining containers in exposed Kubernetes dashboard, consuming cluster resources for weeks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unauthenticated Kubernetes dashboard combined with no resource quotas allowed unlimited pod deployment

real_world_breaches.impact: Service degradation affecting CI/CD pipelines globally for 2 hours due to Kubernetes pod resource saturation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Workload spike without proper resource quotas caused node resource exhaustion

real_world_breaches.impact: Container orchestration infrastructure compromised, resource exhaustion used to mask lateral movement
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of container resource controls allowed attackers to consume resources and evade detection

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Kubernetes cluster DoS via fork bomb in unbounded container, crashed entire production namespace
PID namespace exhaustion attack causing node failure through unlimited process spawning

security_research.title: Kubernetes Security Best Practices: Resource Management
security_research.key_finding: Over 60% of Kubernetes clusters in production lack proper ResourceQuota and LimitRange configurations

security_research.title: The State of Kubernetes Security 2023
security_research.key_finding: Resource limit misconfigurations are the #2 most common Kubernetes security issue after RBAC problems

security_research.title: NIST SP 800-190: Application Container Security Guide
security_research.key_finding: Container resource limits are critical defense against DoS and must be enforced at orchestration layer

mitigations.title: Enforce Namespace ResourceQuota Policies
mitigations.description: Implement mandatory ResourceQuota objects in all namespaces to limit total compute, memory, storage, and object count. This prevents any single namespace from monopolizing cluster resources.

code_example.code:
# Production ResourceQuota for application namespaces
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: production-quota
  namespace: production-apps
spec:
  hard:
    # Compute resource limits
    requests.cpu: "100"           # Total CPU requests across all pods
    requests.memory: "200Gi"      # Total memory requests
    limits.cpu: "200"             # Total CPU limits
    limits.memory: "400Gi"        # Total memory limits
    
    # Storage limits
    requests.storage: "500Gi"     # Total PVC storage
    persistentvolumeclaims: "20"  # Max number of PVCs
    requests.ephemeral-storage: "100Gi"  # Ephemeral storage limit
    limits.ephemeral-storage: "200Gi"
    
    # Object count limits (prevent object sprawl)
    pods: "100"                   # Max pods in namespace
    services: "50"                # Max services
    services.loadbalancers: "5"   # Max LoadBalancer services
    services.nodeports: "10"      # Max NodePort services
    configmaps: "50"              # Max ConfigMaps
    secrets: "50"                 # Max Secrets
    
  # Scope quotas to specific priorities
  scopeSelector:
    matchExpressions:
    - operator: In
      scopeName: PriorityClass
      values: ["high", "medium"]

---
# Development namespace quota (more restrictive)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-quota
  namespace: development
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "20"
    requests.memory: "40Gi"
    limits.cpu: "40"
    limits.memory: "80Gi"
    requests.storage: "100Gi"
    pods: "30"
    services: "20"

---
# Automated ResourceQuota enforcement using admission controller
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-resourcequota
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["v1"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE"]
      resources: ["namespaces"]
  validations:
  - expression: "has(object.metadata.annotations['quota-tier'])"
    message: "Namespace must have quota-tier annotation (small/medium/large)"

---
# Kyverno policy to auto-create ResourceQuota on namespace creation
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-resourcequota
spec:
  background: false
  rules:
  - name: add-quota-to-namespace
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ResourceQuota
      name: default-quota
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      synchronize: true
      data:
        spec:
          hard:
            requests.cpu: "10"
            requests.memory: "20Gi"
            limits.cpu: "20"
            limits.memory: "40Gi"
            pods: "20"

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: kubectl get resourcequota --all-namespaces (verify all namespaces have quotas)
kubectl describe namespace <namespace> (check quota limits)
kubectl get events --field-selector reason=FailedCreate (check for quota violations)
kubectl top nodes (monitor resource usage against quotas)

mitigations.title: Implement LimitRange for Default Container Limits
mitigations.description: Configure LimitRange objects to set default CPU/memory requests and limits for containers that don't specify them. This ensures no container can run unbounded.

code_example.code:
# Comprehensive LimitRange for production namespace
apiVersion: v1
kind: LimitRange
metadata:
  name: production-limitrange
  namespace: production-apps
spec:
  limits:
  # Container-level limits
  - type: Container
    default:  # Default limits (if not specified)
      cpu: "500m"
      memory: "512Mi"
      ephemeral-storage: "2Gi"
    defaultRequest:  # Default requests (if not specified)
      cpu: "100m"
      memory: "128Mi"
      ephemeral-storage: "1Gi"
    max:  # Maximum allowed
      cpu: "2"
      memory: "4Gi"
      ephemeral-storage: "10Gi"
    min:  # Minimum required
      cpu: "50m"
      memory: "64Mi"
      ephemeral-storage: "500Mi"
    maxLimitRequestRatio:  # Limit cannot exceed request by more than this ratio
      cpu: "4"      # Limit can be max 4x request
      memory: "4"
      ephemeral-storage: "4"
  
  # Pod-level limits (total across all containers)
  - type: Pod
    max:
      cpu: "8"
      memory: "16Gi"
      ephemeral-storage: "40Gi"
    min:
      cpu: "100m"
      memory: "128Mi"
  
  # PVC storage limits
  - type: PersistentVolumeClaim
    max:
      storage: "100Gi"
    min:
      storage: "1Gi"

---
# Strict LimitRange for untrusted workloads
apiVersion: v1
kind: LimitRange
metadata:
  name: untrusted-limitrange
  namespace: sandbox
spec:
  limits:
  - type: Container
    default:
      cpu: "200m"
      memory: "256Mi"
    defaultRequest:
      cpu: "50m"
      memory: "64Mi"
    max:
      cpu: "500m"      # Severely restricted
      memory: "512Mi"
    maxLimitRequestRatio:
      cpu: "2"         # Tight ratio
      memory: "2"

---
# Kyverno policy to block pods without resource requests/limits
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-resource-limits
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: check-container-resources
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "All containers must have CPU and memory requests and limits defined"
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
  
  - name: check-init-containers
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Init containers must have resource limits"
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(initContainers):
          - resources:
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"

validation_steps: kubectl get limitrange --all-namespaces
kubectl describe limitrange -n <namespace>
Deploy test pod without limits and verify defaults are applied
Try to exceed max limits and verify rejection

mitigations.title: Enable Pod Security Standards (Baseline/Restricted)
mitigations.description: Enforce Kubernetes Pod Security Standards to prevent dangerous configurations including unlimited resources, privileged containers, and host namespace access.

code_example.code:
# Enable Pod Security Admission at cluster level
# Add to kube-apiserver flags:
# --enable-admission-plugins=PodSecurity
# --admission-control-config-file=/etc/kubernetes/admission-config.yaml

# /etc/kubernetes/admission-config.yaml
apiVersion: apiserver.config.k8s.io/v1
kind: AdmissionConfiguration
plugins:
- name: PodSecurity
  configuration:
    apiVersion: pod-security.admission.config.k8s.io/v1
    kind: PodSecurityConfiguration
    defaults:
      enforce: "restricted"         # Default to most restrictive
      enforce-version: "latest"
      audit: "restricted"
      audit-version: "latest"
      warn: "restricted"
      warn-version: "latest"
    exemptions:
      usernames: []
      runtimeClasses: []
      namespaces: ["kube-system", "kube-node-lease", "kube-public"]

---
# Apply Pod Security Standards to namespaces via labels
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: production-apps
  labels:
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version: v1.27
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit: restricted
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn: restricted

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: legacy-apps
  labels:
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: baseline
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version: v1.27
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit: restricted
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn: restricted

---
# OPA Gatekeeper constraint for Pod Security
apiVersion: templates.gatekeeper.sh/v1
kind: ConstraintTemplate
metadata:
  name: k8spspresourcelimits
spec:
  crd:
    spec:
      names:
        kind: K8sPSPResourceLimits
  targets:
    - target: admission.k8s.gatekeeper.sh
      rego: |
        package k8spspresourcelimits
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          container := input.review.object.spec.containers[_]
          not container.resources.limits.cpu
          msg := sprintf("Container %v has no CPU limit", [container.name])
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          container := input.review.object.spec.containers[_]
          not container.resources.limits.memory
          msg := sprintf("Container %v has no memory limit", [container.name])
        }
        
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          container := input.review.object.spec.containers[_]
          cpu_limit := container.resources.limits.cpu
          cpu_limit_num := units.parse_bytes(cpu_limit)
          cpu_limit_num > 4000000000  # 4 cores
          msg := sprintf("Container %v CPU limit exceeds 4 cores", [container.name])
        }

---
apiVersion: constraints.gatekeeper.sh/v1beta1
kind: K8sPSPResourceLimits
metadata:
  name: must-have-resource-limits
spec:
  enforcementAction: deny
  match:
    kinds:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        kinds: ["Pod"]
    excludedNamespaces:
      - kube-system
      - kube-node-lease

---
# Prevent dangerous sysctls and host access
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-host-access
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  rules:
  - name: prevent-host-namespaces
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Pods cannot use host namespaces (PID, IPC, Network)"
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(hostPID): false
          =(hostIPC): false
          =(hostNetwork): false
  
  - name: prevent-privileged
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Privileged containers are not allowed"
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - =(securityContext):
              =(privileged): false
  
  - name: prevent-host-paths
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Host path volumes are not allowed"
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
          - X(hostPath): "null"

validation_steps: kubectl label namespace <ns> pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted
kubectl auth can-i create pods --as=system:serviceaccount:<ns>:default -n <ns>
Deploy test pod with privileged: true and verify rejection
kubectl get psp (if using legacy PodSecurityPolicy) or check PSS labels

mitigations.title: Prevent Fork Bombs with PID Limits
mitigations.description: Configure kernel PID limits and Kubernetes PID resource limits to prevent process explosion attacks that exhaust the process table.

code_example.code:
# Enable PID limiting at kubelet level (kubelet config)
apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: KubeletConfiguration
featureGates:
  SupportPodPidsLimit: true
  SupportNodePidsLimit: true
podPidsLimit: 1024              # Max PIDs per pod
evictionHard:
  pid.available: "1000"         # Evict pods when node PIDs < 1000

---
# Set PID limits in Pod spec
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: safe-pod
  namespace: production-apps
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: myapp:latest
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "128Mi"
      limits:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "512Mi"
        # PID limit (requires kernel support)
        pids: "256"  # Maximum 256 processes in container
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 1000
      capabilities:
        drop:
          - ALL
      seccompProfile:
        type: RuntimeDefault

---
# RuntimeClass with PID cgroup limits
apiVersion: node.k8s.io/v1
kind: RuntimeClass
metadata:
  name: limited-runtime
handler: runc
overhead:
  podFixed:
    memory: "20Mi"
    cpu: "10m"
scheduling:
  nodeSelector:
    node.kubernetes.io/instance-type: standard
# Configure runc/containerd with PID limits in /etc/containerd/config.toml:
# [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".containerd.runtimes.runc.options]
#   SystemdCgroup = true
#   [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".containerd.runtimes.runc.options.Resources]
#     PidsLimit = 1024

---
# Kyverno policy to enforce PID limits
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: enforce-pid-limits
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  rules:
  - name: check-pid-limit
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Containers must specify PID limits"
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              limits:
                pids: "?*"

---
# Node-level PID limit via DaemonSet (sysctl configuration)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: set-pid-limits
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: pid-limit-setter
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: pid-limit-setter
    spec:
      hostPID: true
      hostNetwork: true
      initContainers:
      - name: set-sysctl
        image: busybox:latest
        command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - |
          # Set kernel PID max
          sysctl -w kernel.pid_max=65536
          
          # Set max user processes
          echo "* soft nproc 4096" >> /host/etc/security/limits.conf
          echo "* hard nproc 8192" >> /host/etc/security/limits.conf
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
        volumeMounts:
        - name: host-etc
          mountPath: /host/etc
      containers:
      - name: pause
        image: gcr.io/google-containers/pause:3.2
      volumes:
      - name: host-etc
        hostPath:
          path: /etc
          type: Directory
      tolerations:
      - effect: NoSchedule
        operator: Exists

---
# Example: Fork bomb prevention test
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: forkbomb-test
  namespace: sandbox
spec:
  containers:
  - name: test
    image: ubuntu:22.04
    command: ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
    args:
    - |
      # This fork bomb should be limited by PID constraints
      # bomb() { bomb | bomb & }; bomb
      echo "Testing PID limits..."
      ulimit -u 256  # Userspace protection
      # Attempt fork bomb (will fail due to limits)
      bash -c 'fork() { fork | fork & }; fork' || echo "Fork bomb blocked by PID limit"
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "128Mi"
        pids: "128"
    securityContext:
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 65534

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
validation_steps: kubectl exec <pod> -- cat /sys/fs/cgroup/pids/pids.max
kubectl exec <pod> -- sh -c 'ulimit -u'
Deploy fork bomb test pod and verify PID limit enforcement
Check kubelet logs for PID eviction events

mitigations.title: Limit Ephemeral Storage and /dev/shm
mitigations.description: Configure ephemeral storage limits and shared memory (/dev/shm) size to prevent disk exhaustion attacks through temporary file creation or memory-backed filesystem abuse.

code_example.code:
# Pod with ephemeral storage and /dev/shm limits
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: storage-limited-pod
  namespace: production-apps
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "128Mi"
        ephemeral-storage: "1Gi"     # Minimum ephemeral storage
      limits:
        cpu: "500m"
        memory: "512Mi"
        ephemeral-storage: "5Gi"     # Maximum ephemeral storage
    volumeMounts:
    - name: cache
      mountPath: /tmp/cache
    - name: dshm
      mountPath: /dev/shm
  volumes:
  - name: cache
    emptyDir:
      sizeLimit: "2Gi"               # Limit EmptyDir volume size
  - name: dshm
    emptyDir:
      medium: Memory                 # /dev/shm uses RAM
      sizeLimit: "128Mi"             # Limit shared memory to 128MB

---
# Kyverno policy to enforce ephemeral storage limits
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-ephemeral-storage-limits
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  rules:
  - name: check-ephemeral-storage
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "All containers must define ephemeral-storage requests and limits"
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              requests:
                ephemeral-storage: "?*"
              limits:
                ephemeral-storage: "?*"
  
  - name: limit-emptydir-size
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "EmptyDir volumes must specify sizeLimit"
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
          - =(emptyDir):
              sizeLimit: "?*"

---
# Kubelet configuration for ephemeral storage management
apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: KubeletConfiguration
evictionHard:
  memory.available: "500Mi"
  nodefs.available: "10%"            # Evict when node disk < 10%
  nodefs.inodesFree: "5%"            # Evict when inodes < 5%
  imagefs.available: "15%"           # Evict when image storage < 15%
evictionSoft:
  memory.available: "1Gi"
  nodefs.available: "15%"
  nodefs.inodesFree: "10%"
  imagefs.available: "20%"
evictionSoftGracePeriod:
  memory.available: "1m30s"
  nodefs.available: "2m"
  nodefs.inodesFree: "2m"
  imagefs.available: "2m"
evictionMaxPodGracePeriod: 60
imageGCHighThresholdPercent: 85     # Start image GC at 85% disk
imageGCLowThresholdPercent: 80      # Image GC target

---
# DaemonSet to monitor and alert on storage abuse
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: storage-monitor
  namespace: kube-system
data:
  monitor.sh: |
    #!/bin/bash
    # Monitor ephemeral storage usage
    while true; do
      # Check /dev/shm usage
      for pod_shm in /var/lib/kubelet/pods/*/volumes/kubernetes.io~empty-dir/dshm; do
        if [ -d "$pod_shm" ]; then
          usage=$(du -sm "$pod_shm" | cut -f1)
          if [ $usage -gt 500 ]; then  # Alert if > 500MB
            echo "ALERT: High /dev/shm usage in $pod_shm: ${usage}MB"
          fi
        fi
      done
      
      # Check ephemeral storage per container
      for container_log in /var/log/containers/*.log; do
        size=$(du -m "$container_log" | cut -f1)
        if [ $size -gt 1000 ]; then  # Alert if logs > 1GB
          echo "ALERT: Large container log: $container_log (${size}MB)"
        fi
      done
      
      sleep 60
    done

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: storage-monitor
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: storage-monitor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: storage-monitor
    spec:
      hostPID: true
      containers:
      - name: monitor
        image: busybox:latest
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
        args:
        - |
          while true; do
            df -h /host/var/lib/kubelet
            df -h /host/var/log
            sleep 300
          done
        volumeMounts:
        - name: kubelet
          mountPath: /host/var/lib/kubelet
          readOnly: true
        - name: logs
          mountPath: /host/var/log
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: kubelet
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/kubelet
      - name: logs
        hostPath:
          path: /var/log
      tolerations:
      - effect: NoSchedule
        operator: Exists

validation_steps: kubectl exec <pod> -- df -h /tmp
kubectl exec <pod> -- df -h /dev/shm
kubectl describe node <node> | grep -A 10 'Allocated resources'
Test: Fill /dev/shm and verify eviction triggers

indicators: Sudden spike in pod evictions across multiple nodes
High CPU or memory usage on nodes with no corresponding application load
API server latency increases or timeouts during normal operations
Unexpected namespace creation or pod deployment by service accounts
Containers running with unlimited resource requests/limits
Rapid growth in ephemeral storage or /dev/shm usage

log_queries.query:
// Detect resource exhaustion attacks in AKS
AzureDiagnostics
| where Category == "kube-audit" or Category == "kube-apiserver"
| where log_s contains "ResourceQuotaExceeded" or log_s contains "LimitRangeExceeded"
| extend podName = extract(@"pod\":\"([^\"]+)", 1, log_s)
| extend namespace = extract(@"namespace\":\"([^\"]+)", 1, log_s)
| summarize AttemptCount = count() by namespace, podName, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where AttemptCount > 10
| order by AttemptCount desc

// Detect pods without resource limits
| union (
  AzureDiagnostics
  | where Category == "kube-audit"
  | where OperationName == "pods/create"
  | extend hasLimits = log_s contains '"limits":{"cpu"' and log_s contains '"limits":{"memory"'
  | where hasLimits == false
  | project TimeGenerated, Namespace = extract(@"namespace\":\"([^\"]+)", 1, log_s), 
            PodName = extract(@"name\":\"([^\"]+)", 1, log_s)
)

// Detect node resource pressure
| union (
  KubeNodeInventory
  | where Status contains "DiskPressure" or Status contains "MemoryPressure" or Status contains "PIDPressure"
  | summarize PressureEvents = count() by Computer, Status, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
  | where PressureEvents > 3
)

index=kubernetes sourcetype=kube:apiserver
("ResourceQuotaExceeded" OR "LimitRangeExceeded" OR "OutOfPids" OR "DiskPressure")
| rex field=_raw "namespace\":\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)"
| rex field=_raw "pod\":\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)"
| stats count by namespace, pod, _time
| where count > 10

# Detect pods created without resource limits
| append [
  search index=kubernetes sourcetype=kube:apiserver verb="create" objectRef.resource="pods"
  | spath output=cpu_limit path=requestObject.spec.containers{}.resources.limits.cpu
  | spath output=mem_limit path=requestObject.spec.containers{}.resources.limits.memory
  | where isnull(cpu_limit) OR isnull(mem_limit)
  | stats count by objectRef.namespace, objectRef.name, user.username
]

# Detect fork bomb patterns (high process count)
| append [
  search index=kubernetes sourcetype=kube:container:logs "fork" OR "Cannot fork"
  | stats count by kubernetes.pod_name, kubernetes.namespace_name
  | where count > 50
]

// Resource quota violations
event.dataset: "kubernetes.audit" AND kubernetes.audit.verb: "create" AND 
  kubernetes.audit.responseStatus.code: 403 AND 
  kubernetes.audit.responseStatus.reason: ("ResourceQuotaExceeded" OR "LimitRangeExceeded")
| stats count by kubernetes.audit.objectRef.namespace, kubernetes.audit.objectRef.name

// Pods without resource limits (from admission logs)
OR (
  event.dataset: "kubernetes.audit" AND 
  kubernetes.audit.verb: "create" AND 
  kubernetes.audit.objectRef.resource: "pods" AND 
  NOT kubernetes.audit.requestObject.spec.containers.resources.limits.cpu: * AND
  NOT kubernetes.audit.requestObject.spec.containers.resources.limits.memory: *
)

// Node resource pressure
OR (
  kubernetes.node.status.condition.type: ("DiskPressure" OR "MemoryPressure" OR "PIDPressure") AND
  kubernetes.node.status.condition.status: "True"
)

# EKS cluster resource exhaustion
fields @timestamp, kubernetes.pod_name, kubernetes.namespace_name, container_cpu_usage_total, container_memory_working_set_bytes
| filter kubernetes.namespace_name not like /kube-/
| stats max(container_cpu_usage_total) as max_cpu, 
        max(container_memory_working_set_bytes) as max_mem,
        count(*) as pod_count by kubernetes.namespace_name
| filter max_cpu > 3000000000 or max_mem > 4000000000

# Detect resource quota exceeded errors
| union (
  fields @message, @timestamp
  | filter @message like /ResourceQuotaExceeded|LimitRangeExceeded/
  | parse @message /namespace["\s:]+(?<namespace>[^\s,"]+)/
  | stats count() as violation_count by namespace, bin(5m)
  | filter violation_count > 5
)

# Node pressure conditions
| union (
  fields node_status.conditions.type, node_status.conditions.status, node_name
  | filter node_status.conditions.type in ["DiskPressure", "MemoryPressure", "PIDPressure"]
  | filter node_status.conditions.status = "True"
  | stats count() by node_name, node_status.conditions.type
)

# CPU throttling detection (containers hitting limits)
sum by (namespace, pod, container) (
  rate(container_cpu_cfs_throttled_seconds_total[5m])
) > 0.1

# Memory usage near limits
sum by (namespace, pod, container) (
  container_memory_working_set_bytes / container_spec_memory_limit_bytes
) > 0.9

# Pods without resource limits
count by (namespace, pod) (
  kube_pod_container_resource_limits{resource="cpu"} == 0 or 
  kube_pod_container_resource_limits{resource="memory"} == 0
)

# Node resource pressure
kube_node_status_condition{condition=~"DiskPressure|MemoryPressure|PIDPressure", status="true"} == 1

# Namespace quota usage
sum by (namespace, resource) (
  kube_resourcequota{type="used"} / kube_resourcequota{type="hard"}
) > 0.8

# Pod evictions
rate(kube_pod_status_reason{reason="Evicted"}[5m]) > 0

tools.name: Falco
tools.usage: Detects fork bombs, high process counts, and resource abuse in containers

tools.name: Prometheus + Alertmanager
tools.usage: Monitor resource quotas, CPU throttling, memory pressure, and eviction rates

tools.name: Kubescape
tools.usage: Scan clusters for missing ResourceQuota, LimitRange, and Pod Security configurations

tools.name: Polaris
tools.usage: Audit workloads for resource limit configurations and security best practices
